questionText: A curve has parametric equations $x = \sqrt{t}$ and $y = 4t - 5$, where $t \geq 0$. Find a Cartesian equation of the curve, and state the domain of $x$ and the range of $y$.

steps.description: Rearrange the root equation for t
steps.explanation: Squaring both sides undoes the square root and gives $t$ in terms of $x$. Remember that $\sqrt{t}$ denotes the non-negative root, a fact we will use for the domain.

steps.description: Substitute t into the y equation
steps.explanation: Replacing $t$ with $x^{2}$ removes the parameter and gives a relation between $x$ and $y$ only.
This is an upward parabola, but the parametrisation only traces part of it, which we pin down next.
A real square root is never negative, so $x$ can never be negative no matter how large $t$ becomes.
Only non-negative $x$ appears, so the domain of the curve is $x \geq 0$: just the right-hand half of the parabola.

steps.description: Find the smallest value of y
steps.explanation: On the interval $x \geq 0$ the term $4x^{2}$ is smallest at $x = 0$, giving the lowest $y$-value of $-5$.

steps.description: Note the behaviour as x grows
steps.explanation: As $x$ increases without bound, $4x^{2}$ dominates and $y$ grows without limit, so there is no upper bound.
The values of $y$ start at $-5$ (achieved at $x = 0$) and increase from there, so the range is $y \geq -5$.

steps.description: Check the endpoint from the parameter
steps.explanation: At $t = 0$ the parametric equations give the point $(0,\,-5)$, matching the corner of our domain and range.
The Cartesian equation together with the domain and range fully describes the traced curve.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: $y = 4x^{2} - 5$ for $x \geq 0$ (the right-hand half of the parabola); the range is $y \geq -5$.
